Combined contour plot for pancreas and liver


```{r}
# Load required packages
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# =============================================================================
# PANCREAS DATA PROCESSING
# =============================================================================

# Define the file paths for pancreas (Ctrl and T1D groups)
files_pancreas <- c("/work/of21_work/pancreas_cpact_pathways.csv",
                    "/work/of21_work/pancreas_ORA_pathways_ctrl.csv",
                    "/work/of21_work/pancreas_AUCell_pathways_ctrl.csv",
                    "/work/of21_work/pancreas_scGSEA_pathways_ctrl.csv",
                    "/work/of21_work/pancreas_ORA_pathways_t1d.csv",
                    "/work/of21_work/pancreas_AUCell_pathways_t1d.csv",
                    "/work/of21_work/pancreas_scGSEA_pathways_t1d.csv")

# Read each CSV file into a list and combine by rows
df_list_pancreas <- lapply(files_pancreas, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
combined_df_pancreas <- do.call(rbind, df_list_pancreas)

# Define the desired pathways (lists for Ctrl and T1D)
ctrl_active_pathways <- c("Autophagy", 
    "Protein digestion and absorption",
    "PI3K-Akt signaling pathway",
    "Insulin signaling pathway",
    "Oxidative phosphorylation",
    "Protein processing in endoplasmic reticulum",
    "mTOR signaling pathway",
    "HIF-1 signaling pathway",
    "AMPK signaling pathway",
    "PPAR signaling pathway",
    "cGMP-PKG signaling pathway")

ctrl_inactive_pathways <- c("Salmonella infection",
    "Bacterial invasion of epithelial cells",
    "Pathogenic Escherichia coli infection",
    "Thyroid cancer",
    "Shigellosis",
    "Apoptosis",
    "Cytokine-cytokine receptor interaction")


desired_pathways <- c(ctrl_active_pathways, ctrl_inactive_pathways)

# Filter the combined data for only the desired pathways
filtered_df_pancreas <- combined_df_pancreas[combined_df_pancreas$Pathway.Name %in% desired_pathways, ]

# Add a ground truth column:
# For Ctrl: +1 if pathway is in ctrl_active_pathways, -1 if in ctrl_inactive_pathways.
# For T1D: +1 if pathway is in t1d_active_pathways, -1 if in t1d_inactive_pathways.
final_combined_df_pancreas <- filtered_df_pancreas %>%
  mutate(ground_truth = case_when(
    Group == "Ctrl" & Pathway.Name %in% ctrl_active_pathways ~ 1,
    Group == "Ctrl" & Pathway.Name %in% ctrl_inactive_pathways ~ -1,
    Group == "T1D" & Pathway.Name %in% t1d_active_pathways ~ 1,
    Group == "T1D" & Pathway.Name %in% t1d_inactive_pathways ~ -1,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ground_truth))

# Compute precision, recall, and F1 for each method in pancreas data
method_summary_pancreas <- final_combined_df_pancreas %>%
  group_by(Type) %>%
  summarise(
    TP = sum(Value == 1 & ground_truth == 1),
    FP = sum(Value == 1 & ground_truth == -1),
    FN = sum(Value == -1 & ground_truth == 1)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    precision = ifelse((TP + FP) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FP)),
    recall    = ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FN)),
    F1        = ifelse((precision + recall) == 0, 0, 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall))
  )

# =============================================================================
# LIVER DATA PROCESSING
# =============================================================================

# Define the file paths for liver data
files_liver <- c("/work/of21_work/hepatocyte_cpact_pathways.csv",
                 "/work/of21_work/hepatocyte_ORA_pathways.csv",
                 "/work/of21_work/hepatocyte_AUCell_pathways.csv",
                 "/work/of21_work/hepatocyte_scGSEA_pathways.csv")

# Read each CSV file into a list and combine
df_list_liver <- lapply(files_liver, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
final_combined_df_liver <- do.call(rbind, df_list_liver)

# Clean pathway names: change any starting with "Drug metabolism" to a unified name
final_combined_df_liver$Pathway.Name <- ifelse(grepl("^Drug metabolism", final_combined_df_liver$Pathway.Name),
                                               "Drug metabolism - cytochrome P450",
                                               final_combined_df_liver$Pathway.Name)

# Define pathway lists for liver data
metabolic_pathways <- c("Biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids", "Carbon metabolism", 
  "Biosynthesis of amino acids", "Biosynthesis of cofactors",
  "Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis", "Pentose and glucuronate interconversions",
  "Ascorbate and aldarate metabolism", "Pyruvate metabolism", "Fatty acid degradation",
  "Primary bile acid biosynthesis", "Steroid hormone biosynthesis",
  "Arachidonic acid metabolism", "Linoleic acid metabolism",
  "Glycine, serine and threonine metabolism",
  "Cysteine and methionine metabolism", "Tyrosine metabolism",
  "Taurine and hypotaurine metabolism", "Retinol metabolism",
  "Porphyrin metabolism", "Metabolism of xenobiotics by cytochrome P450",
  "Cholesterol metabolism", "Caffeine metabolism", "Drug metabolism - cytochrome P450")

cancer_pathways <- c("Thyroid cancer", "Shigellosis", "Colorectal cancer",
    "Pancreatic cancer", "Hepatocellular carcinoma", "Gastric cancer",
    "Glioma", "Acute myeloid leukemia", "Chronic myeloid leukemia",
    "Basal cell carcinoma", "Melanoma", "Renal cell carcinoma",
    "Bladder cancer", "Prostate cancer", "Endometrial cancer",
    "Breast cancer", "Small cell lung cancer", "Non-small cell lung cancer")

# Add ground truth for liver: +1 for metabolic pathways and -1 for cancer pathways.
final_combined_df_liver <- final_combined_df_liver %>%
  mutate(ground_truth = case_when(
    Pathway.Name %in% metabolic_pathways ~ 1,
    Pathway.Name %in% cancer_pathways ~ -1,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ground_truth))

# Compute precision, recall, and F1 for each method in liver data
method_summary_liver <- final_combined_df_liver %>%
  group_by(Type) %>%
  summarise(
    TP = sum(Value == 1 & ground_truth == 1),
    FP = sum(Value == 1 & ground_truth == -1),
    FN = sum(Value == -1 & ground_truth == 1)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    precision = ifelse((TP + FP) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FP)),
    recall    = ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FN)),
    F1        = ifelse((precision + recall) == 0, 0, 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall))
  )

# =============================================================================
# COMBINE SUMMARIES AND PREPARE FOR PLOTTING
# =============================================================================

# Add a Tissue column and a combined label for the legend.
method_summary_liver <- method_summary_liver %>%
  mutate(Tissue = "Liver",
         MethodLabel = paste0(Type, " (Liver)"))
method_summary_pancreas <- method_summary_pancreas %>%
  mutate(Tissue = "Pancreas",
         MethodLabel = paste0(Type, " (Pancreas)"))

# Combine the two summaries into one data frame
combined_summary <- bind_rows(method_summary_liver, method_summary_pancreas)

# Reorder factor levels so that each method’s Liver and Pancreas entries are consecutive:
combined_summary$MethodLabel <- factor(combined_summary$MethodLabel, 
  levels = c("CPACT (Liver)", "CPACT (Pancreas)",
             "ORA (Liver)", "ORA (Pancreas)",
             "AUCell (Liver)", "AUCell (Pancreas)",
             "scGSEA (Liver)", "scGSEA (Pancreas)"))

# =============================================================================
# CREATE PRECISION-RECALL GRID FOR THE CONTOUR PLOT
# =============================================================================

precision_vals <- seq(0.01, 1, length.out = 100)
recall_vals    <- seq(0.01, 1, length.out = 100)
grid <- expand.grid(precision = precision_vals, recall = recall_vals)
grid$F1 <- with(grid, 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall))

# Define contour levels and annotation labels for selected F1 values
F1_levels <- seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1)
annot_levels <- c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9)
offset <- -0.035
annot_df <- data.frame(
  x = annot_levels + offset,
  y = annot_levels + offset,
  label = paste0("F1=", annot_levels)
)

# =============================================================================
# CREATE THE COMBINED CONTOUR PLOT WITH A TWO-COLUMN LEGEND ARRANGED BY ROW
# =============================================================================

ggplot() +
  # Draw thicker F1 contour lines
  geom_contour(data = grid, aes(x = precision, y = recall, z = F1),
               breaks = F1_levels, color = "grey50", size = 1.2) +
  # Annotate selected F1 levels
  geom_text(data = annot_df, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
            color = "black", size = 11, fontface = "bold", vjust = 0.65) +
  # Overlay the method points with color and shape determined by MethodLabel
  geom_point(data = combined_summary,
             aes(x = precision, y = recall, color = MethodLabel, shape = MethodLabel),
             size = 10) +
  labs(x = "Precision",
       y = "Recall",
       title = "") +
  # Use the same legend title so that color and shape legends merge
  scale_color_manual(name = NULL, values = c(
    "CPACT (Liver)"    = "#FF1F58",
    "CPACT (Pancreas)" = "#FF1F58",
    "ORA (Liver)"      = "#00CD6C",
    "ORA (Pancreas)"   = "#00CD6C",
    "AUCell (Liver)"   = "#009ADE",
    "AUCell (Pancreas)"= "#009ADE",
    "scGSEA (Liver)"   = "#AF58BA",
    "scGSEA (Pancreas)"= "#AF58BA"
  )) +
  scale_shape_manual(name = NULL, values = c(
    "CPACT (Liver)"    = 17,  # triangle
    "CPACT (Pancreas)" = 16,  # circle
    "ORA (Liver)"      = 17,
    "ORA (Pancreas)"   = 16,
    "AUCell (Liver)"   = 17,
    "AUCell (Pancreas)"= 16,
    "scGSEA (Liver)"   = 17,
    "scGSEA (Pancreas)"= 16
  )) +
  # Set legend to display in 2 columns arranged by row (so that each method appears in one row)
  guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE),
         shape = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.01), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     labels = function(x) ifelse(x == 0, "", x),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     labels = function(x) sub("\\.0$", "", x),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 23),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 23),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 42),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 42),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 40),
        # Position the legend inside the plot; adjust coordinates as needed.
        legend.position = c(0.7, 0.31),
        legend.key.size = unit(1.8, "lines"),              # Increase legend key size

        # Optional: add a semi-transparent white background for clarity.
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.6), color = NA))

# Save the plot
#ggsave("/work/of21_work/combined_precision_recall_plot.jpeg", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600)


```
